new_classes.label_en: Semi-Sparkling Wine in Containers of More Than 2 Litres (CN 22042910)
new_classes.label_de: Perlwein in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 Litern (KN 22042910)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes, other than sparkling wine of subheading 2204 10, in containers of more than 2 litres, presented either in bottles with mushroom stoppers held in place by ties or fastenings, or otherwise put up with an excess pressure due to carbon dioxide in solution of not less than 1 bar but less than 3 bar measured at 20 °C; classified under CN code 2204 29 10 (Chapter 22).

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) wine other than subheading 2204 10 (sparkling wine) – modelled via disjointness complement from SparklingWine; (2) container volume > 2 litres – modelled via decimalRange minExclusive 2 on maxContainerVolumeL (this is the 2204 29 branch); (3) excess pressure ≥ 1 bar and < 3 bar at 20 °C – modelled via two decimalRange restrictions on excessPressureBar; (4) the mushroom-stopper presentation is a specific packaging form that constitutes an alternative criterion to the pressure range for the same code — this nuance (two alternative definitional criteria: mushroom stopper OR pressure 1–3 bar) is only partially expressible in OWL without a disjunction at the restriction level, which reduces coverage slightly. The 20 °C temperature measurement condition for the pressure cannot be directly expressed as an OWL datatype restriction, leaving a small gap.
